name: silent-skip-branches-are-ambiguous
tags: logging, observability, decision points, fast path, skip branch, debug level, silent failure
body:
A high-frequency "fast skip" branch (fires on nearly every event/tool-call/prompt) with zero log output at ANY level is indistinguishable from that code path never running at all. This is a distinct problem from unlogged exceptions — the code isn't failing, it's just invisible, so a real regression (e.g. a routing bug that makes every call take the skip path when it shouldn't) produces the exact same silence as correct behavior.

**Why:** Audited gate_check.py, cache_check.py, activate_task.py (task:9cec0cae, 2026-07-05) and found each had at least one "nothing to do here" branch with no log call — cache_check.py logged HIT but never MISS, so a cache that silently stopped matching anything would look identical to a cache that was never being checked. hooks/gates.py, by contrast, already logged every allow/deny/fail-open path from the start and needed no changes — it's the reference example.

**How to apply:** When auditing a module for observability, don't just check exception handlers — check whether every meaningful branch of an if/else decision point logs its outcome, including the "took no action" branches. Use `debug` level (not `info`/`warning`) for branches that fire on every event to avoid log noise — the goal is visibility-on-demand, not a wall of INFO spam. A silent branch that fires rarely (once per session, once per error) is lower priority than one that fires on every single call.
